title: Backrooms
review: I thought it wasn't as suspenseful as they might have hoped, because every moment of tension was the same: creeping into a strange new room, over and over. Clark was a good character, and even though silently exploring a mysterious room sounds like a cringeworthy assignment in an amateur acting class, he pulls it off in a compelling way. It's such a simple movie that I don't have much else to say about it. I liked the ambiguous ending that raises just enough questions about the personal and emotional attributes of the Backrooms, not explaining anything, but letting you know it's more than just a soulless pocket dimension. There's a level near the end of {Half-Life: Alyx} which is like a warped liminal space, similar in a way to the Backrooms, and it's my favourite part of that amazing game.
